summary: This metric hit a record low during the COVID-19 pandemic (2020), when children's asthma-related ER visits fell because people stayed indoors, reducing exposure to outdoor asthma triggers, and health care utilization patterns shifted generally. As pandemic conditions eased, visits rose for three straight years, though the Health Department notes the rate remains below its pre-pandemic (2019) level and represents a decline over the past 15 years overall.
oneLiner: increasing as pandemic-era lows reverse, though still below the 2019 level
